families.rationale: Command vocabularies and workflow grouping may become closed contract language; durable command services and session stores remain operations-owned.

families.operations_handoff: Mutable holder execution, publication, cleanup, rollback, retained-resource, and concurrency behavior remain operations/cpk-server proof.
families.rationale: The 23 mutation-holder laws are split-boundary laws: extracted core maps the closed EnvironmentContract / RuntimeContract / DerivedResource vocabulary, descriptor no-value/no-secret law, and operations-owned handoff in #791; operations / cpk-server must later prove executable holder mutation, publication, cleanup, rollback, retained-resource, and concurrency behavior.

families.operations_handoff: Operation session execution, action ordering, and graph-truth non-mutation remain operations/cpk-server proof.
families.rationale: These laws execute Postgres-backed operation workflow services. #787 maps the command vocabulary; #791 maps persistence boundary contracts; #792 records the executable session service, action ordering, and graph-truth non-mutation behavior as operations/cpk-server handoff rather than core implementation.
families.reviewed_supersession: parity-manifest entries carry reviewed #792 supersession records

laws: Every #740 family must be mapped, explicitly split with operations handoff, or reviewed as operations/cpk-server handoff.
Split-boundary laws are not counted as executable operations behavior completed in core.
Workflow service execution laws are superseded only as core obligations and remain operations/cpk-server proof obligations.
No Postgres, UnitOfWork, mutable holder, route, or runtime-effect implementation moves into extracted core.
The live required-core inventory must contain no unexpected #740 family after closeout.
